notes: This was an extremely difficult image for all 15 model variants — NONE correctly identified the left 10th posterior rib fracture (the load-bearing finding). Per the rubric, this caps all scores at 3-5. Only qwen3.5-4b|think scored above 3: it uniquely localized an abnormality on the CORRECT side (right of image, matching the fracture location), though it mischaracterized it as 'patchy opacities / atelectasis' rather than a fracture. Several models (glm-4.6v-flash-9b, qwen3-vl-4b, qwen3-vl-8b, qwen3.5-4b|nothink, qwen3.5-9b|nothink, qwen3.6-27b|nothink, qwen3.6-35b-a3b|nothink) made ACTIVE false-negative claims explicitly stating 'no fractures' or 'ribs intact' — these represent confident wrong diagnoses and are scored at 3 (no bonus for clean structure). Models that simply described anatomy without asserting fracture absence (gemma4 family, qwen3.5-9b|think, qwen3.6-27b|think, qwen3.6-35b-a3b|think) are also scored at 3 but ranked slightly higher for not actively misrepresenting the finding. The |nothink variants tended to be more confidently wrong (more explicit false negatives and demographic hallucinations) than their |think counterparts. Common hallucinations: gastric bubble, aortic knob specifics, humerus heads at image corners (actually scapulae), breast-shadow-based sex inference.
